encounter.reason: Asthma exacerbation triggered by cold air and occupational dust exposure
encounter.location: Cascade Community Health Center, 8205 SE Foster Road, Portland, OR 97206

screening.name: Asthma Control Test score
screening.note: Retrospectively estimated for the prior 2-week period

medications.name: albuterol sulfate 90 mcg/actuation HFA inhaler
medications.sig: 2 puffs inhaled every 4-6 hours as needed for wheezing or shortness of breath
medications.reason: mild asthma exacerbation; underlying mild intermittent asthma

document_references.description: Telemedicine video visit note documenting asthma exacerbation evaluation. Patient reports 10 days of increased albuterol use (daily, sometimes twice daily) associated with cold weather and occupational dust exposure. ACT score 18. Assessment: mild exacerbation, no infection. Plan: continue albuterol PRN, environmental modification counseling (cold air protection, occupational dust mitigation), return precautions for worsening, telephone follow-up in 7 days.
